mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over a 6-month period signals rising inflation expectations, leading to higher nominal interest rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 5-day forward returns in high-inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decline in the unemployment rate over a 3-month period signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher wage growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over a 6-month period signals rising economic activity, leading to higher demand for raw materials and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or 60-day forward returns in high-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle, leading to higher borrowing costs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, but only when the unemployment rate is below 4%.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with low unemployment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 20-day forward returns in tightening monetary cycle periods with low unemployment are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in housing starts (HOUST) over a 3-month period signals rising housing demand, leading to higher economic activity and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, but only when the Consumer Price Index (CPI) is below 2%.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or 20-day forward returns in high-growth periods with low inflation are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% decline in the unemployment rate over a 3-month period signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher wage growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, but only when the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) is above 2%.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with high economic activity
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods with high economic activity are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% decline in the RSAFS (Real State and Local Government Finances) over a 6-month period signals reduced government spending, leading to lower economic activity and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days, but only when the federal funds rate is above 2%.
regime_hint: late-cycle contraction with high interest rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 60-day forward returns in low-growth periods with high interest rates are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
